series_title: Black Gold Empires: The Men Who Bled the Earth Dry
writer_persona: a brilliant scriptwriter for high-end, HBO-style documentaries, weaving forensic history with the tension of a psychological thriller
style_guidelines: Write in vivid, cinematic prose that treats each magnate's life like a Shakespearean tragedy of ambition
Ground every claim in real historical events, deals, and documented eccentricities — no invented dialogue passed off as fact
Build tension through pacing: slow-burn intimacy in quiet moments, breathless urgency at turning points
Treat oil and mineral wealth as both a divine gift and a corrupting poison — let that irony bleed into every section
Avoid glorifying ruthlessness; expose the human cost behind every barrel and pipeline
Use sensory, geographic detail — deserts, refineries, boardrooms, tankers — to make abstract wealth feel physical
End each episode on a haunting note that lingers, not a tidy moral

audio.default_style: Read like an audiobook narrator, calm, steady, and engaging

series_intro: Open on the smell of crude in the desert wind — a low, resonant narration describing how beneath the sands of empires and the ice of tundras lay a substance that would rewrite the map of human power. Introduce the anthology's central thesis: that oil and mineral wealth do not merely enrich men, they consume them, turning visionaries into obsessives who reroute rivers, topple governments, and warp the very geography of the earth in pursuit of liquid gold — only to find themselves drowning in the very fortunes they built.
series_outro: A final, philosophical meditation delivered in a hushed, weighted tone: after all the pipelines, the coups, the fortunes bought and lost, ask what remains when the wells run dry and the men who commanded empires of oil are reduced to dust like the very ground they drilled. Reflect on how each magnate believed he was mastering the earth, when in truth the earth was patiently waiting to reclaim him — leaving behind only rusted derricks, forgotten contracts, and the eternal, echoing question of whether any fortune was ever worth the soul it cost.

episodes.theme: The reclusive Armenian financier known as 'Mr. Five Percent' engineered the secret oil partitioning of the collapsing Ottoman Empire, amassing one of history's greatest personal fortunes while retreating into a gilded, paranoid isolation of art and obsession that outlived the empires he helped carve apart.
episodes.outro_note: As Gulbenkian's fortune calcifies into silence behind museum walls, the narrative turns from the shadows of Constantinople to the roaring tanker decks of London — where another man was about to prove that oil wasn't just found in the ground, but won on the high seas.
sections: The Hook & The Man Who Owned Five Percent of the World's Oil
The Rising Conflict / Carving the Ottoman Carcass
The Obsession / The Red Line Agreement and the Art of Secrecy
The Point of No Return / Exile, War, and the Hoarding of Masterpieces
The Legacy & The Recluse Who Left an Empire of Silence
section_styles: Read like an audiobook narrator, calm, steady, and engaging
Whisper softly and gently, close to the microphone
Speak fearfully, with short breaths and suspense
Speak with a sad, empathetic and heavy tone

episodes.theme: A London seashell trader's son transformed a family curiosity shop into the Shell oil empire, defying the Suez Canal's rigid rules and Rockefeller's American monopoly by pioneering the first oil tankers — reshaping global shipping geography itself, only to watch his creation slip from his grasp in a financial storm of his own making.
episodes.intro_note: From Gulbenkian's guarded vaults of art and silence, the story surfaces into open water — to a man who bet everything on steel hulls and lamp oil, determined to break a monopoly that spanned an ocean.
episodes.outro_note: Shell's tankers now crisscross the globe under a name that outlived its founder — but across the Atlantic, a very different kind of oilman was learning that the deadliest deals aren't signed with governments, but with dictators.
sections: The Hook & The Seashell Merchant's Impossible Gamble
The Rising Conflict / Breaking the Suez Canal's Deadly Rules
The Obsession / Outrunning Rockefeller Across the Oceans
The Point of No Return / The Panic That Nearly Sank an Empire
The Legacy & The Shell That Outlasted the Man
section_styles: Read like an audiobook narrator, calm, steady, and engaging
Speak fast, energetic, and with sharp articulation
Speak with great excitement and passion
Speak fearfully, with short breaths and suspense
Speak with a sad, empathetic and heavy tone

episodes.theme: The American industrialist and self-styled statesman built Occidental Petroleum from a shell of a company into an oil giant by striking audacious, morally murky deals with Muammar Gaddafi's Libya and the Soviet Union, blending genuine ambition with rumored corruption until his obsession with legacy and art collecting consumed the very empire he'd built on foreign oil fields.
episodes.intro_note: From the storm-tossed tankers of Shell, the story crosses an ocean and half a century — to a man who understood that in the new age of oil, the most valuable currency wasn't crude itself, but the ear of dictators.
sections: The Hook & The Doctor Who Became an Oil Baron
The Rising Conflict / Charming the Kremlin and the Colonel
The Obsession / The Libyan Gamble That Broke the Cartel
The Point of No Return / Scandal, Suspicion, and the Cost of Access
The Legacy & The Empire Sold for a Legend
